mission.title: Install and Configure Slimfaas on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimFaas is a lightweight, plug-and-play Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) platform for Kubernetes. It is designed to be fast, simple, and extremely slim, making it an excellent choice for deploying serverless functions in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the SlimFaas Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the SlimFaas Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add slimfaas https://charts.slimfaas.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SlimFaas using Helm
steps.description:
Install SlimFaas in a new namespace called 'slimfaas' with the following command:
```bash
helm install slimfaas slimfaas/slimfaas --namespace slimfaas --create-namespace --version 0.61.14
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the SlimFaas pods to ensure they are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace slimfaas
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the SlimFaas deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment slimfaas --namespace slimfaas
```
Add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Expose SlimFaas service
steps.description:
Expose the SlimFaas service to access it externally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/slimfaas 8080:80 --namespace slimfaas
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SlimFaas will have the pods running in the 'slimfaas' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080. Additionally, resource limits will be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add slimfaas https://charts.slimfaas.dev
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the SlimFaas release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall slimfaas --namespace slimfaas
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation. This ensures that no leftover resources remain in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd slimfaasfunctions.slimfaas.dev
kubectl delete crd slimfaasfunctionruntimes.slimfaas.dev
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace slimfaas
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'slimfaas' namespace to clean up any remaining resources. After deletion, verify that the namespace has been removed.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace slimfaas
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's important to back up the current state of your SlimFaas installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace slimfaas -o yaml > slimfaas-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the SlimFaas Helm repository and run the upgrade command to apply the latest version. Ensure you specify the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade slimfaas slimfaas/slimfaas --namespace slimfaas --version 0.61.15
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After upgrading, check the status of the SlimFaas pods to ensure they are running the new version without issues.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace slimfaas
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your SlimFaas pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. This can indicate issues with the application configuration or resource limits.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace slimfaas
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the SlimFaas service, ensure that the service is running and the port-forwarding command is active. Check the service status and logs.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace slimfaas
kubectl port-forward service/slimfaas 8080:80 --namespace slimfaas
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to insufficient resources, check the resource limits set in the deployment and the available resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment slimfaas --namespace slimfaas
kubectl get nodes --namespace slimfaas
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration errors
troubleshooting.description:
If there are issues with the configuration, review the deployment configuration and ensure all required parameters are set correctly. You can edit the deployment to fix any issues.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment slimfaas --namespace slimfaas
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
